title: Earnings: As of March 31, 2026
content:
The credit union reported net income of $143K as of March 31, 2026. This figure reflects the continued strength of our core lending and investment activities, which remain central to our financial model despite external volatility. Interest income totaled $1,212K while interest expense amounted to $300K, generating spread income that underscores the durability of our primary earnings engine even as market conditions have shifted.

Non-interest income totaled $254K and continues to provide a modest but reliable complement to core earnings. The majority of this income is generated from fee income ($137K), with additional contributions from other non-interest sources ($96K) that support our diversified revenue structure.

Non-interest expense totaled $920K, reflecting continued discipline in managing operating costs while maintaining the quality of services we provide to members. The largest component is credit loss expense ($104K), followed by office occupancy and professional fees (combined at approximately $816K) that align with our investment in member service quality.

Overall Position:
The credit union continues to generate resilient earnings supported by interest income as the dominant driver of profitability. Non-interest income provides a steady complement, while expense levels remain managed investments rather than burdens on performance. Sustaining this trajectory will depend on maintaining disciplined cost management and leveraging our core lending activities into the next period.
